Interview Questions for Candidate
WHY are you looking for a new position? (tell me more)
Here you are looking for what their reason is. Were they fired? Not passionate
about current position? Or maybe they will ‘blame’ the company they are at.
You do not want that.
What attracted you to our company enough to attend an interview?
Here you are looking for what their real WHY is. If they are a true believer in
your company, they will say something to attest to wanting to be a part of the
culture you are building/your cause/your purpose.
What is your biggest weakness? (what else?)
BELIEVE PEOPLE WHEN THEY TELL YOU WHO THEY ARE. Don’t take this
question for granted.
Tell me what your ideal job looks like.... (what else?)
Pay close attention to if the ideal job aligns with what you are offering them. Is
it the same hours? Department? Client contact? Fast vs. slow paced?
What do you enjoy doing in your free time? (what else?)
See who they are. People should let loose on this question and it will give you
some insight into who they are as a person or if they are someone who is so
obsessive about their work that that IS their hobby. 🙂
What kind of hours are you looking for?
Again, we want to make sure they are willing to work minimum hours. This
helps align expectations.
What is your minimum required income?
DO NOT SKIP THIS. People will often take a job for less than they need to get
by because they *hope* or even EXPECT a raise early on. This is a missing
piece in many managers communication and it needs to be addressed head
on. They cannot operate fully in their position if they are functioning from a
place of scarcity.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 1 --
